purpose: Close the fallback candidate universe over EVERY previously-characterized solver profile (owner review correction 1). Existing pre-quarantine characterization evidence only; NO new solver runs.

F_Q.solver: QUADPROG_SQRT (the primary; not a fallback candidate)

eligibility_filter: algorithmically distinct from the active-set primary
judged by the identical external certifier
selection_rule: among admissible profiles: minimize |F_Q cap F_Pj| (primary); tie-break lower standalone |F_Pj|; then resource; then deterministic id

exclusion_reasons: EXCLUDED: invalid dual mapping — conic dual cannot survive 1/sqrt(t) (commit 18c55f5; remediation manifest MR002_QP_CandidateCapabilityManifest_ClarabelRemediation.json). Primal correct to 1.7e-13 but the dual certificate collapses (amplification up to ~1e4) -> fails 'valid primal AND dual mappings' / common-certifier eligibility
EXCLUDED: not algorithmically distinct from the active-set primary QUADPROG_SQRT (same solver family)
EXCLUDED: not algorithmically distinct from the active-set primary QUADPROG_SQRT (same solver family)
EXCLUDED: not algorithmically distinct from the active-set primary QUADPROG_SQRT (same solver family)

resolution.clarabel_note: CLARABEL has the LOWEST standalone count (29 < 51) but is eliminated on the PRIMARY criterion: it rescues only 4/5 of F_Q (U=1), so it never reaches the tie-break. It is ALSO inadmissible (invalid dual mapping). Its low standalone count is correctly overridden by incomplete conditional coverage.
resolution.winner_basis: among admissible interior-point profiles that rescue all 5 F_Q (U=0), PIQP_P2 (51) < PIQP_P1 (59) on the standalone tie-break
resolution.stop_condition_not_triggered: no admissible candidate beats PIQP_P2 on the frozen rule; the outcome holds over the closed universe, not merely the two PIQP profiles
